---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size50[.center[

Review our risk aversion cases and brainstorm strategies for addressing these complications

]]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for Class
1. Check citations on shared Google sheet.




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Write a report analyzing your environmental problem in terms of its complications.

In what specific ways does risk, uncertainty, collective action problems and unequal impacts complicate your problem?

+ Support all claims with evidence
]

???

So, keep your eye on the prize.

We're building up to writing paper 2.

The collected citations for today (and each week in section 2) offer you evidence you will need for that paper! 



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

.size60[**For Today**]

.size40[
Find us a **recent, real world example** of differing levels of **risk aversion** complicating environmental problem-solving. 

Look for examples with the sides evaluating the risks of the problem very differently. 

Be ready to help us **brainstorm strategies** for addressing these complications.
]

???

### Everybody ready to present?

Review and discuss cases

Clarify concepts

<br>

Your Example: The (possibly) false promise of net zero pledges
- Net Zero is Fake Zero: why we need real emissions reductions, not gimmicks and false promises
- Net zero carbon pledges have good intentions. But they are not enough.
- The hollow promise of net-zero carbon pledges
- The False Promise of Net Zero
- Also, Jared Diamond's "Collapse" Prologue (Mistakes are inadvertent, 12 categories of “ecocide” and five lessons to learn)




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-green_blue_swirl_side.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

class: middle

# Paper 2
.size45[
Write a report analyzing your environmental problem in terms of its complications.

In what specific ways does risk, uncertainty, collective action problems and unequal impacts complicate your problem?

+ Support all claims with evidence
]

???

Connect to your ongoing projects: think about all the cases we've heard today and tell us in what specific ways does your environmental problem present these complications?

Which pieces of evidence most useful for you?



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/05-1-risk-cartoon.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

.size40[
.center[
.content-box-blue[**Strategies for Stakeholders with**]

.content-box-blue[**Different Risk Profiles?**]
]]

???

Brainstorm strategies for addressing these complications.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-gentle_lines_green.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle, center

.size60[**Next Week's Complication**]

<br>

.size40[
Uncertainty, Time Horizons and Discount Rates
]

???

### Questions?
